mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as cost of capital falls.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in deteriorating corporate earnings and recession risks over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, driving positive equity returns as corporate revenue growth accelerates over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization without overheating, supporting sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL stability, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate profit expectations, producing positive short-term equity returns as market participants adjust valuations upward within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows or 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 260, inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of observations or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, rising housing starts amplify the wealth effect and construction spending, generating positive equity returns over the next month as credit conditions improve.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when HOUST rises during easing periods, or the signal loses predictive power in a regime-switching model.
